member.name: Josh Dean
member.synopsis: Josh Dean is the Labour MP for Hertford and Stortford , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure that the new initial teacher training and early career framework includes content related to (a) identifying children’s social and emotional developmental needs and (b) suppor
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Initial Teacher Training (ITT) and Early Career Framework covers the first three years or more of a teacher’s career. It sets out a minimum entitlement to training for all new teachers and, following a review, now contains significantly more content r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to encourage and incentivise mainstream schools to use data-driven assessment tools to identify children’s hidden social, emotional, and mental health needs before they escal
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is supporting mainstream schools to take a more evidence‑based approach to early identification of need, including social, emotional and mental health needs.We are developing National Inclusion Standards, backed by up to £15 million, which ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupil Exclusions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether Ofsted or another regulatory body will be mandated to track the destination outcomes of children placed in school inclusion bases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Inclusion bases will have specialisms, providing tailored and expert teaching and support for specific groups of children. We know there are lots of great examples of mainstream schools delivering specialist provision through inclusion bases, enabling chi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she is taking steps to help ensure that the proposed school inclusion bases are designed as integrated, short-term bridges back to mainstream education rather than pathways toward permanent exclusion.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Inclusion bases will have specialisms, providing tailored and expert teaching and support for specific groups of children. We know there are lots of great examples of mainstream schools delivering specialist provision through inclusion bases, enabling chi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps he is taking to support individuals over 50 back into employment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I provided on 17 March 2026 to question: UIN 119633.

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment Schemes: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to support people over the age of 50 out of work to return to work.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Work helps everyone play active and fulfilling roles in society while building financial security for retirement, and we recognise the wealth of skills and experience that older workers bring both to the workplace and the economy. The Department is commit...

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether her Department is taking steps to increase revenue funding for youth services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: ‘Youth Matters: Your National Youth Strategy’ is a 10 year plan to ensure every young person across the country has somewhere to go, someone who cares for them and a community they feel part of.The strategy is supported by more than £500 million in fundin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether she plans to introduce new duties on councils to deliver youth services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the National Youth Strategy, we will explore reviewing the current local authorities’ statutory duty for youth services and how to empower local authorities to better deliver on their responsibilities for youth services.DCMS recognises the i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Young People: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what discussions she had with Cabinet colleagues on the implementation of the National Youth Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 10th December 2025, we published ‘Youth Matters’, the first cross-government Strategy for young people in England in 15 years. Backed by £500m of DCMS funding over the next 3 years, the Strategy will ensure every young person has somewhere to go, someo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Young People: Departmental Coordination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to help support cross-Departmental coordination of the implementation of the National Youth Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Increasing support for young people to improve opportunities is a shared mission across Government. The National Youth Strategy led by DCMS and co-designed by young people and the youth sector, will set a cross-government direction for the next decade to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: Local Press
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what recent assessment his Department has made on the potential merits of using (a) local and (b) regional newspapers for government advertising.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government selects media channels for communications based on reaching target audiences effectively. The Government values the important role of local and regional newspapers and is developing a Local Media Strategy. As part of this, the Cabinet Offic...

writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the responsibility of the integrated care boards (ICBs) in England to make appropriate provision to meet the health and care needs of their local population, including providing access to attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) assessment an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Young People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what recent progress her Department has made on developing the National Youth Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State was pleased to announce the new National Youth Strategy, stating the Department’s commitment to delivering a strategy by and for young people through the most ambitious listening exercise in a generation. We have launched our nation...
